title: Error: qemu exited unexpectedly with exit code 1, stderr: qemu-system-x86_64: -netdev stream,id=vlan,server=off,addr.type=unix,addr.path=/run/user/1000/podman/podman-machine-default-gvproxy.sock: Parameter 'type' does not accept value 'stream'
body:
### Issue Description

I use a github repository source for deployment, and I follow the script below:


After running `podman init` successfully, the following error occurs during `podman start` :

```
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"
Error: qemu exited unexpectedly with exit code 1, stderr: qemu-system-x86_64: -netdev stream,id=vlan,server=off,addr.type=unix,addr.path=/run/user/1000/podman/podman-machine-default-gvproxy.sock: Parameter 'type' does not accept value 'stream'
```



### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue

```
#!/bin/bash

# Podman
# Install golang
sudo snap install go --classic

# install by origin source
sudo apt update

sudo apt-get install \
  btrfs-progs \
  crun \
  git \
  go-md2man \
  iptables \
  libassuan-dev \
  libbtrfs-dev \
  libc6-dev \
  libdevmapper-dev \
  libglib2.0-dev \
  libgpgme-dev \
  libgpg-error-dev \
  libprotobuf-dev \
  libprotobuf-c-dev \
  libseccomp-dev \
  libselinux1-dev \
  libsystemd-dev \
  make \
  pkg-config \
  uidmap -y -f

# install podman
git clone https://github.com/containers/podman.git
cd podman
make
sudo make install
cd ..

sudo groupadd podman
sudo usermod -aG podman $USER

# install cargo
curl https://sh.rustup.rs -sSf | sh

# install netavark
git clone https://github.com/containers/netavark.git
cd netavark
make
sudo make install
cd ..

# install plugins
git clone https://github.com/containernetworking/plugins.git
cd plugins
./build_linux.sh
sudo chown root:root bin/*
sudo chmod 655 bin/*
sudo cp bin/* /usr/bin/
cd ..

# install gvproxy
git clone https://github.com/containers/gvisor-tap-vsock.git
cd gvisor-tap-vsock
make
sudo chown root:root bin/*
sudo chmod 655 bin/*
sudo cp bin/* /usr/libexec/podman/
cd ..

# install virtiofsd
git clone https://gitlab.com/virtio-fs/virtiofsd.git
cd virtiofsd
apt install libcap-ng-dev libseccomp-dev
cargo build --release
sudo chown root:root target/release/virtiofsd
sudo chmod 655 target/release/virtiofsd
sudo cp target/release/virtiofsd /usr/bin/
cd ..

sudo apt install conntrack -y -f
# sudo apt install podman-docker -y -f
# sudo apt install podman-compose -y -f
# sudo apt install pipx -y -f

python3 -m pip install podman-compose --break-system-packages --upgrade
sudo python3 -m pip install podman-compose --break-system-packages --upgrade

# cri-o

sudo apt install apt-transport-https ca-certificates curl gnupg gnupg2 software-properties-common -y
export KUBERNETES_VERSION=v1.30
export CRIO_VERSION=v1.30
echo "deb [signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/kubernetes-apt-keyring.gpg] https://pkgs.k8s.io/core:/stable:/$KUBERNETES_VERSION/deb/ /" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/kubernetes.list
echo "deb [signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/cri-o-apt-keyring.gpg] https://pkgs.k8s.io/addons:/cri-o:/stable:/$CRIO_VERSION/deb/ /" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/cri-o.list

sudo rm -rf /etc/apt/keyrings/kubernetes-apt-keyring.gpg
curl -fsSL https://pkgs.k8s.io/core:/stable:/$KUBERNETES_VERSION/deb/Release.key | sudo gpg --dearmor -o /etc/apt/keyrings/kubernetes-apt-keyring.gpg
sudo rm -rf /etc/apt/keyrings/cri-o-apt-keyring.gpg
curl -fsSL https://pkgs.k8s.io/addons:/cri-o:/stable:/$CRIO_VERSION/deb/Release.key | sudo gpg --dearmor -o /etc/apt/keyrings/cri-o-apt-keyring.gpg

sudo apt update
sudo apt install kubelet kubeadm kubectl -y
sudo apt install cri-o -y -f
# sudo apt install cri-o-runc -y -f
cd ~/Git/Development-Environment-Build/packaged/
# sudo dpkg -i containernetworking-plugins_1.1.1+ds1-3build1_amd64.deb
sudo apt install containernetworking-plugins -y -f
sudo apt install cri-tools -y -f
sudo systemctl daemon-reload
sudo systemctl enable crio
sudo systemctl start crio
sudo systemctl restart crio

sudo swapoff -a
sudo modprobe br_netfilter
sudo sysctl -w net.ipv4.ip_forward=1

kubeadm init
podman machine init
podman machine start

sudo adduser $USER podman
sudo groupadd podman
sudo usermod -aG podman $USER

```

the following error occurs during `podman start` 

### Describe the results you received

After running `podman init` successfully, the following error occurs during `podman start` :

```
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"
Error: qemu exited unexpectedly with exit code 1, stderr: qemu-system-x86_64: -netdev stream,id=vlan,server=off,addr.type=unix,addr.path=/run/user/1000/podman/podman-machine-default-gvproxy.sock: Parameter 'type' does not accept value 'stream'
```



### Describe the results you expected

Describe the results you expected

successfully executed

```
Error: podman-machine-default: VM does not exist
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

Use my computer Ubuntu 22.04.


### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

At the moment, I have searched the internet and information, but I can't find any way to help me solve the problem, or some of my own knowledge is not clear, so I hope it can help me.

comments.body:
Podman requires a newer version of QEMU, you need 7.2 or later (like in Ubuntu 24.04*) since 656bf7b76447b2b14eb73be4497ef407180cf45b.

\* https://packages.ubuntu.com/qemu-system
As mentioned you need a newer qemu. Is there a reason you are trying to use podman machine on linux? You can simply use podman natively without all the VM overhead.

The `podman machine start`  was probably meant to be `systemctl --system enable --now podman.socket`, the docs are "hidden":

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/tutorials/remote_client.md

The "podman group" stuff is also not complete, it is missing a few key steps (and so doesn't **do** anything, like it did with docker)

You can still use `sudo podman --remote` though, and similar root ssh connections.

Thanks for your replies and suggestions, I'm using Ubuntu 22.04 version, so I can't get Podman 4+ or above, so I deployed it myself. But if I encounter any QEMU problems in the future, I'll come back to check out the instructions, thanks!

I rebooted my computer and it worked. I'm not sure why, but I've reorganized my scripts in the hope that I can add them to the manual installation and deployment section of the official manual.

Podman 4+ and above I found that I can logout without stopping the service, so I can remove or modify the systemctl service that I designed before.

```bash
#!/bin/bash

# Podman
# Install golang
sudo snap install go --classic

# install by origin source
sudo apt update

sudo apt install \
  btrfs-progs \
  doxygen \
  git \
  go-md2man \
  iptables \
  libassuan-dev \
  libbtrfs-dev \
  libc6-dev \
  libdevmapper-dev \
  libglib2.0-dev \
  libgpgme-dev \
  libgpg-error-dev \
  libprotobuf-dev \
  libprotobuf-c-dev \
  libseccomp-dev \
  libselinux1-dev \
  libsystemd-dev \
  make \
  pkg-config \
  libcap-dev \
  uidmap -y -f

# install podman
git clone https://github.com/containers/podman.git
cd podman
make
sudo make install
cd ..

sudo groupadd podman
sudo usermod -aG podman $USER

# install cargo
curl https://sh.rustup.rs -sSf | sh

# install netavark
git clone https://github.com/containers/netavark.git
cd netavark
make
sudo make install
cd ..

# install plugins
git clone https://github.com/containernetworking/plugins.git
cd plugins
./build_linux.sh
sudo chown root:root bin/*
sudo chmod 655 bin/*
sudo cp bin/* /usr/bin/
cd ..

# install gvproxy
git clone https://github.com/containers/gvisor-tap-vsock.git
cd gvisor-tap-vsock
make
sudo chown root:root bin/*
sudo chmod 655 bin/*
sudo cp bin/* /usr/bin/
cd ..

# install virtiofsd
git clone https://gitlab.com/virtio-fs/virtiofsd.git
cd virtiofsd
apt install libcap-ng-dev libseccomp-dev
cargo build --release
sudo chown root:root target/release/virtiofsd
sudo chmod 655 target/release/virtiofsd
sudo cp target/release/virtiofsd /usr/bin/
cd ..

# install aardvark-dns
git clone https://github.com/containers/aardvark-dns.git
cd aardvark-dns
make
sudo make install
sudo chown root:root bin/*
sudo chmod 655 bin/*
sudo cp bin/* /usr/bin/
cd ..

# install passt
git clone https://passt.top/passt
cd passt
make
sudo make install
cd ..

# install yajl
git clone https://github.com/lloyd/yajl.git
cd yajl
./configure
make
sudo make install
cd ..

# install crun
git clone https://github.com/containers/crun.git
cd crun
./autogen.sh
./configure
make
sudo make install
./autogen.sh && ./configure
make
sudo make install
cd ..

sudo apt install conntrack -y -f

python3 -m pip install podman-compose --break-system-packages --upgrade
sudo python3 -m pip install podman-compose --break-system-packages --upgrade

# cri-o

sudo apt install apt-transport-https ca-certificates curl gnupg gnupg2 software-properties-common -y
export KUBERNETES_VERSION=v1.30
export CRIO_VERSION=v1.30
echo "deb [signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/kubernetes-apt-keyring.gpg] https://pkgs.k8s.io/core:/stable:/$KUBERNETES_VERSION/deb/ /" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/kubernetes.list
echo "deb [signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/cri-o-apt-keyring.gpg] https://pkgs.k8s.io/addons:/cri-o:/stable:/$CRIO_VERSION/deb/ /" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/cri-o.list

sudo rm -rf /etc/apt/keyrings/kubernetes-apt-keyring.gpg
curl -fsSL https://pkgs.k8s.io/core:/stable:/$KUBERNETES_VERSION/deb/Release.key | sudo gpg --dearmor -o /etc/apt/keyrings/kubernetes-apt-keyring.gpg
sudo rm -rf /etc/apt/keyrings/cri-o-apt-keyring.gpg
curl -fsSL https://pkgs.k8s.io/addons:/cri-o:/stable:/$CRIO_VERSION/deb/Release.key | sudo gpg --dearmor -o /etc/apt/keyrings/cri-o-apt-keyring.gpg

sudo apt update
sudo apt install kubelet kubeadm kubectl -y
sudo apt install cri-o -y -f
# sudo apt install cri-o-runc -y -f
cd ~/Git/Development-Environment-Build/packaged/
# sudo dpkg -i containernetworking-plugins_1.1.1+ds1-3build1_amd64.deb
sudo apt install containernetworking-plugins -y -f
sudo apt install cri-tools -y -f
sudo systemctl daemon-reload
sudo systemctl enable crio
sudo systemctl start crio
sudo systemctl restart crio

sudo swapoff -a
sudo modprobe br_netfilter
sudo sysctl -w net.ipv4.ip_forward=1

kubeadm init
podman machine init
podman machine start

sudo adduser $USER podman
sudo groupadd podman
sudo usermod -aG podman $USER
```

我雖然沒有使用 `podman machine start` ，但仍然可以使用 podman 相關功能，所以暫時應該不會去處理 VM 版本問題，待後續處理。

I'm not using `podman machine start`, but I can still use podman functions, so I shouldn't have to deal with the VM version issue for now, I'll deal with it later.
